design_philosophy.core_loop: Launch ball, navigate pinball table physics, score points through bumpers and ramps, keep ball in play
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Ball control and trajectory prediction against gravity and table geometry; maintaining ball in play vs. drains
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master realistic pinball table mechanics with four distinct themed environments; develop skill at digital pinball simulation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to realistic physics simulation constrained to elements buildable in real pinball machines; rejects arcade abstractions in favor of authentic pinball feel

difficulty.lives_system: Balls per game (standard pinball format); multiple tables allow skill progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High skill ceiling through mastery of table-specific ramp routes and bumper sequences; player controls flipper timing and ball trajectory

progression.unlock_mechanism: Four distinct pinball tables selectable from menu: Ignition (space), Steel Wheel (western), Beat Box (music industry), Nightmare (graveyard)
replayability_hooks: Multiple tables with distinct themes and rule sets
Score chasing across four different layouts
Table-specific advanced features (noted as non-functional bug on Amiga 1200 Beat Box)

world_building_techniques: Table theming through visual design (rocket/planets for Ignition, steam trains for Steel Wheel, music industry for Beat Box, graveyard for Nightmare)
Contextual aesthetic choices (Nightmare table inspired by Terminator 2 pinball machine released 1991)
Regional customization (European version features Munch Bunch characters)

danger_signals: Nightmare table's graveyard aesthetic with ghosts, demons, and evil iconography (blood changed to blue censorship in SNES port, crosses removed)
environmental_storytelling: Ignition: space exploration theme with rocket launch and planetary elements
Steel Wheel: Old West setting with steam train aesthetic
Beat Box: Music industry environment with charts, bands, and tours
Nightmare: Graveyard setting grounded in real horror/supernatural imagery

sources_used: Wikipedia - Pinball Dreams article (gameplay section, development details, reception, legacy)
The Cutting Room Floor - Pinball Dreams (SNES) article (regional differences, port information)
